agent_notes: Kingston has several PAINTS-suitable cultural anchors, but the same-category backup rule is the binding constraint, especially for museums/galleries and north Kingston pubs.
No Creative Walks were included because no PAINTS-approved park/route from the v6 safety list sits clearly within the Kingston upon Thames district; Bushy Park is nearby but not a Kingston pick.
Operational details should be host-confirmed before publishing, especially group walk-in tolerance and bank-holiday hours.

venues.opening_hours: Official/listing hours show Tue-Wed 9:30am-6pm, Thu 9:30am-9pm, Fri-Sat 9:30am-11pm, Sun 10am-6pm, Mon closed. Bank holiday handling: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Appears to operate as wine shop/cafe/bar with drink-in service; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable at off-peak daytime or early evening, but host should call ahead for 8-12.
venues.capacity_notes: Small shop-bar format; avoid Friday/Saturday peak if no table arrangement is agreed.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 5:30pm-7:30pm or Saturday late afternoon before peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: A wine merchant that functions as a cafe and bar gives people something to bring to show-and-tell: bottles, labels, regions, and taste notes. The Market Place setting gives it local texture without being a default high-street chain. Live music and a shop-shelf wine model make it more conversational than a standard pub table.
venues.anecdote_story: ExCellar describes itself as having evolved from wine merchant into wine cafe, with bottles to buy for home or drink in, plus food and live music.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, beer, coffee, charcuterie, cheese, pies, day and evening menus.
venues.safety_notes: Central Kingston, high footfall, close to Kingston rail and bus routes. Evening dispersal should use lit routes to Kingston station.
venues.backup_venue: The Fighting Cocks, about 6-8 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6-8 min walk via Eden Street/Old London Road; host should verify live route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Group walk-in and table seating not published; host confirmation needed before event listing.

venues.opening_hours: Official/Facebook/Instagram signals say open from noon seven days a week; exact closing, bank holiday handling and private-hire conflicts are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bar appears walk-in; gigs/events may be ticketed or crowded. Group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm and avoid headline gig times.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a bolder PAINTS group early evening before ticketed events; poor for quiet conversation during gigs.
venues.capacity_notes: Live-music venue and pub; use early bar slot, not gig room peak.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or Thursday 5pm-7pm before the room gets loud.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the Kingston pick with the most story: independent, alternative, theatrical, and visibly unlike a generic drinks venue. It gives strangers a shared object of attention immediately, from posters and film nights to the local music mythology. The PAINTS caveat is noise: it needs a host-managed early slot.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue claims music history back to the 1930s/40s jazz circuit, a building established in 1890, and links to Kingston's Eel Pie Island-era scene; The Guardian has also profiled it as a key south-west London rock pub.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bar drinks, pub food, gigs, film screenings, comedy and alternative music programming.
venues.safety_notes: Near Kingston station and on a lit town-centre route. Late-night gig crowd may be intense for some attendees; host should define a clear meet/depart point.
venues.backup_venue: The Grey Horse / Ram Jam Records, about 4 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk along Richmond Road; host should verify live route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Obviousness penalty applied for being a known venue with Guardian/Time Out visibility; noise and event ticketing can undermine conversation.

venues.venue_name: The Grey Horse / Ram Jam Records
venues.opening_hours: Official hours list kitchen daily 12pm-10pm; bar Sun-Thu 12pm-11pm, Fri-Sat 12pm-1am. Bank holiday handling: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub/bar walk-ins appear normal; Ram Jam events may be ticketed or full. Group of 8-12 tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better as a drinks-and-music backup than a quiet first-choice PAINTS table.
venues.capacity_notes: Pub plus intimate back-room venue; large group should avoid comedy/live-music peaks unless that is the event focus.
venues.best_time_slot: Tuesday-Thursday 5:30pm-7:30pm, or Wednesday open-mic if host wants structured listening.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Ram Jam back-room gives this pub a cultural reason to exist beyond food and beer. It is a good PAINTS backup for a music-led Show & Tell group, especially if the host frames attendees' objects around songs, gigs, or subcultures. It stays Tier B because conversation conditions depend heavily on programming.
venues.anecdote_story: Creative Youth describes the Grey Horse and Ram Jam as a long-running local treasure, with Ram Jam supporting gigs, open mic nights, dance and fringe theatre.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Bar, BBQ food, whisky, live blues/funk/soul/jazz, open mic, comedy.
venues.safety_notes: Close to Kingston rail with lit town-centre routes. Late events need clear group dispersal plan.
venues.backup_venue: The Fighting Cocks, about 4 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk; host should verify live route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Noise, ticketed shows and comedy sell-outs may reduce walk-in viability.

venues.opening_hours: Official hours show Wed-Sat 12pm-11pm, Sun 12pm-9pm, closed Mon-Tue; CAMRA notes December exceptions. Bank holiday handling: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Book-a-table option exists; walk-in group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Reservation recommended, so capped at Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely comfortable for a pub-table conversation with advance heads-up; walk-in for 8-12 not proven.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood pub with garden and real fires; not ideal for a surprise group at Sunday lunch or Friday dinner.
venues.why_fits_paints: A family-run, owner-operated neighbourhood pub has the warmth PAINTS wants for low-pressure conversation. The cask ale/CAMRA story and traditional-room feel give people tangible prompts without needing a formal activity. It is demoted because no same-category backup clearly clears all gates within 10 minutes.
venues.anecdote_story: The Wych Elm presents itself as an indie owner-operator pub; CAMRA describes its 19th-century building history and beer-quality recognition.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ales, pub food, garden, real fires.
venues.safety_notes: Residential north Kingston route; safe-feeling early evening, but host should keep group departure toward Kingston station together after dark.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying backup found within 10 min - closest qualifying alternatives are The Fighting Cocks/ExCellar in central Kingston at roughly 15-20 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Reservation recommended for a group; no tight PAINTS-grade backup.

venues.opening_hours: Official listing says seven days a week, 10am-5pm; individual stall hours and bank holiday handling are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; N/A for market walk-in. Suggested meeting point: Local Hero, 22 Thames Street, approx. 2 min walk; cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for browsing in pairs/trios, then reconvening at Market Place or nearby independent coffee.
venues.capacity_notes: 28 permanent stalls, street-food scene, central outdoor public realm.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday 11am-1pm or weekday lunch when stalls are open but less crowded.
venues.why_fits_paints: The market has history, food, colour and a natural browsing rhythm for strangers. It supports low-stakes conversation because people can compare finds, street food and old-town details. It is obvious, but genuinely useful as Kingston's central cultural marketplace.
venues.anecdote_story: Kingston's market records date back to the early 1200s; recent local coverage describes it as one of London's oldest markets, opened around 1170.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, fish, bread, flowers, artisan bakery, street food stalls and the Grade II* Market House setting.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall central Kingston, close to rail/bus links. Keep evening versions to lit Market Place/Thames Street routes.
venues.backup_venue: Antiques of Kingston, about 8 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk via Eden Street/Old London Road; host should verify live route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather and stall variation; individual stall closures can change the feel.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: third Saturday of the month, 9am-1pm. 2026 event listings confirm future dates including 18 July, 15 Aug, 19 Sept. Bank holiday handling: N/A unless date shifts; host must confirm monthly date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; N/A for market walk-in. Suggested meeting point: an independent Maple Road cafe/bar within the market street, exact venue and 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a Saturday morning cohort; easy to split and regroup along the pedestrianised market street.
venues.capacity_notes: Up to around 40 stalls; traffic-diverted leafy street format.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the most PAINTS-ready market in the borough: producer-led, local, charitable, and human-scale. The street setting encourages gentle wandering rather than queueing. The mix of food, drink, crafts and volunteer energy gives it a richer story than a standard produce market.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded in 2009, run by volunteers, and profits from stall fees support local charities and good causes; the official site says more than £125,000 has gone back into community projects.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farmers and local artisans, English wine, cheese, charcuterie, cakes, pottery, candles, granola, kombucha and hot food.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime, community-led, near Surbiton station. Use rail-station meet/depart for clarity.
venues.backup_venue: Kingston Ancient Market, approx. 12-15 min by rail/bus/taxi or short transit plus walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Market backup allowed within 15 min transit; host should verify live transit on event day.
venues.risks_watchouts: Only monthly; weather exposure; exact stall mix changes.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Ham Parade; bus 65/371, Kingston/Richmond rail by bus
venues.opening_hours: Official site: first Saturday monthly except January, 10am-2pm; next listed market 4 July 2026. Bank holiday handling: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; N/A for market walk-in. Suggested meeting point: Parade's End Books coffee shop if open or The Bistro nearby; group walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a compact local market, but best with a planned meet point and clear wet-weather fallback.
venues.capacity_notes: Community market with produce, crafts, treats, activities and live music.
venues.why_fits_paints: A community-run market on a local parade has the off-grid quality PAINTS likes. It is less polished than Kingston or Surbiton, but that makes it feel genuinely neighbourhood-led. It stays Tier B because the backup and transport geometry are weaker.
venues.anecdote_story: The market is run by local volunteers and profits go back into improving Ham Parade and supporting the high street.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh local produce, crafts, treats, kids activities, live music.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime, community setting; buses are the main public transport link. Meeting point should avoid ambiguity along the parade.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying market/craft backup found within 15 min walk or transit; closest stronger market options are Kingston Ancient Market or Surbiton Farmers' Market.
venues.risks_watchouts: Borderline district fit because Ham straddles Richmond/Kingston; no qualifying tight backup.

venues.opening_hours: Listing hours show Mon-Fri 10am-6pm, Sat 9:30am-6pm, Sun 10:30am-5pm. Bank holiday handling: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access antiques centre; N/A for market-style browsing. Group of 8-12 entering together is likely acceptable but UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a rainy-day browsing activity in pairs, then regroup outside or at a nearby cafe.
venues.capacity_notes: Indoor multi-dealer antiques/vintage emporium; narrower browsing format than a street market.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is a useful PAINTS backup for object-led conversation: everyone can find a strange, beautiful, or baffling item and explain why it caught them. It is less atmospheric than a street market but far more weather-resilient. Tier B because it is more antiques centre than curated market day.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself as an antiques and vintage emporium with 80+ independent sellers and over 10,000 items.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Antique and vintage jewellery, furniture, mirrors, prints, clothes, decorative objects and collectibles.
venues.safety_notes: Central Kingston, close to station and bus links; indoor daytime browsing is low-risk.
venues.backup_venue: Kingston Ancient Market, about 8 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk; host should verify live route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a classic farmers/craft market; use as object-story session rather than food-led market day.

venues.opening_hours: Current exhibition listing states Knit Wits: Haus of SIBLING runs 12 June-26 September 2026, Thursday-Saturday 10am-5pm. Bank holiday handling and private-hire closures: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum/exhibition appears free/open during hours; timed-entry not indicated. Group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a compact exhibition visit, then cafe/pub debrief nearby.
venues.capacity_notes: Small local museum; group should split while viewing to avoid blocking cases.
venues.why_fits_paints: The current SIBLING knitwear archive gives the visit a genuine exhibition hook, not just local-history filler. Fashion, nightlife ritual and queer/punk visual language all create natural prompts for memory and identity conversations. It is Tier B only because the backup gallery is outside the strict 10-minute rule.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum is a custom-built 1904 civic museum with permanent galleries on Kingston's ancient origins, Town of Kings history, and Eadweard Muybridge.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Knit Wits: Haus of SIBLING archive exhibition; permanent Kingston history and Muybridge displays.
venues.safety_notes: Central civic location close to transit; daytime visit recommended.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - closest strong alternative is Stanley Picker Gallery at roughly 13 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current exhibition press is mostly official/local/social rather than major press; group walk-in should be confirmed.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Kingston School of Art, Grange Road; Kingston rail
venues.opening_hours: New Exhibitions/current listing: Tue-Sat 11am-5pm; current exhibition A Simultaneous Agreement 23 Apr-18 Jul 2026. Bank holiday handling/private closures: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free public gallery; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance and exhibition install gaps are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a thoughtful 60-75 minute gallery session if the host checks install/open status.
venues.capacity_notes: University gallery scale; avoid private-view/event times unless intentionally attending.
venues.why_fits_paints: A university-linked commissioning gallery gives Kingston a contemporary-art edge beyond heritage. The Hogsmill/river-linked programme creates a place-specific frame, which is exactly the kind of detail PAINTS can turn into conversation. It is Tier B because the nearest qualifying backup misses the 10-minute rule.
venues.anecdote_story: Kingston University describes the gallery as a public programme by the Hogsmill River, supported by the Stanley Picker Trust and Arts Council England, connecting the School of Art to wider contemporary practice.
venues.serve_or_on_view: FRAUD: A Simultaneous Agreement through 18 July 2026; linked Hogsmill community/public programme references.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime university/civic setting; route to Kingston station should be kept simple after dark.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying museum/gallery backup found within 10 min - Kingston Museum is roughly 13 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Install periods and university programming changes can close the gallery; host must check the week of event.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Ham Parade; bus 65/371, Kingston/Richmond rail by bus
venues.opening_hours: Instagram/search listings show Mon-Sat 10am-6pm, Sun 11am-5pm; Bookshop Crawl lists the same. Coffee-shop operation and bank holiday handling: UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in appears normal; coffee-shop launch was posted April 2025 but current seating/group capacity is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially lovely for a smaller/quiet group, but 8-12 seating for two hours is unverified; host should cap or arrange in advance.
venues.capacity_notes: Independent bookshop on a local parade; likely compact. Use as browse-and-discuss, not long seated cafe session unless confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: A pandemic-born independent bookshop with a newly signalled coffee offer is exactly the kind of gentle local space PAINTS should notice. It has a literary identity, events, and a founder story. It stays Tier B because the coffee/seating operation is not yet robustly verified from official pages.
venues.anecdote_story: Founder Rachel Thomas opened the shop in 2020 after a long-held dream; local coverage described her as a retired IT worker from Kew who opened the Ham shop during the pandemic.
venues.serve_or_on_view: New fiction, classics, non-fiction, cookery, children's and YA, poetry/art sections; coffee offer reported by the shop's Facebook page.
venues.safety_notes: Quiet daytime parade setting; bus-based transport means host should coordinate arrivals and departures.
venues.backup_venue: Taylor's Fine Wine, about 3-4 min walk, as a quiet independent cultural drinks alternative
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3-4 min walk along Ham Parade/Richmond Road; host should verify live route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee seating and 8-12 tolerance are the largest unknowns; this should not publish without a call.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Beautiful PAINTS fit as Dora Gordine's former studio home, but no clearly current exhibition with recent press was found for the run date; opening/tour format also needs host confirmation. Better as a future pick when a current show is live.

Good community pub and useful local fallback, but current official branding is Young's Pub, which conflicts with the PAINTS no-chain/default-independent rule. Could be reconsidered only if PAINTS relaxes chain ownership for pubs.
Traditional real-ale pub with character, but review-count signal appears below the PAINTS Tier B floor and independent cultural/story evidence is thinner than ExCellar/Fighting Cocks/Wych Elm.

Plausible community-market candidate, but source signals conflict: an old official Square page says cancelled for the foreseeable future while Facebook/event listings suggest activity. Rejected until host can verify current 2026 operation directly.

Strong independent coffee/roastery signal, but no substantive books, resident bookseller, book wall, zine selection or next-door bookshop evidence was found, so it fails the raised Books & Coffee requirement.
Bookshop/cafe model may be operationally easy, but it is a national chain and fails PAINTS' independent/no-chain aesthetic filter.

Bushy Park is PAINTS Premium safety-listed, but it sits outside the Kingston upon Thames borough/district boundary for this assignment. No Creative Walk was included rather than stretching the district definition.
